controls.rationale: Separate pod AWS permissions from node credentials by enabling IRSA federation.
controls.boundarySummary: Allow the named actors to perform the intended action only through the controlled workflow; deny bypasses, broad privileges, and unreviewed changes.
controls.implementedSummary: The EKS module creates an IAM OIDC provider from the cluster issuer and restricts client_id_list to sts.amazonaws.com for IRSA use.

evidence.summary: EKS module creates the IAM OIDC provider and exposes OIDC outputs.
OIDC provider outputs support downstream IRSA roles.

controls.title: EKS OIDC provider enables pod-to-AWS federation
controls.objective: Separate pod AWS permissions from node credentials by enabling IRSA federation.

controls.rationale: Prefer private or tightly scoped Kubernetes API access while acknowledging dev runner access constraints.
controls.boundarySummary: Allow the named actors to perform the intended action only through the controlled workflow; deny bypasses, broad privileges, and unreviewed changes.
controls.implementedSummary: EKS private endpoint access is enabled.
controls.gapSummary: Accepted risk: dev variables still allow public endpoint access from 0.0.0.0/0 for GitHub-hosted runners and workstation access rather than restricting to /32 or private runners.

evidence.summary: Dev endpoint CIDR settings capture accepted public-access tradeoff.
EKS module configures public and private endpoint access.
Network redesign ADR discusses private networking direction.

controls.title: EKS API public endpoint risk is accepted for dev
controls.objective: Prefer private or tightly scoped Kubernetes API access while acknowledging dev runner access constraints.

controls.rationale: Allow one controller service account to reconcile ALB and Gateway resources without giving broad AWS access to workloads.
controls.boundarySummary: Allow the named actors to perform the intended action only through the controlled workflow; deny bypasses, broad privileges, and unreviewed changes.
controls.implementedSummary: IRSA trust is locked to kube-system/aws-load-balancer-controller and includes aud/sub conditions.
controls.gapSummary: The ELB/EC2 controller policy still requires many Resource='*' operations due to AWS API constraints.

evidence.summary: Module defines the controller IRSA trust and IAM policy.
Gateway infrastructure depends on the controller boundary.

controls.title: AWS Load Balancer Controller uses dedicated IRSA
controls.objective: Allow one controller service account to reconcile ALB and Gateway resources without giving broad AWS access to workloads.

controls.rationale: Allow DNS automation only for the authorized domain and hosted zone with explicit ownership controls.
controls.boundarySummary: Allow the named actors to perform the intended action only through the controlled workflow; deny bypasses, broad privileges, and unreviewed changes.
controls.implementedSummary: IRSA trust is locked to external-dns/external-dns; Route 53 mutation is limited to the configured hosted zone with domain filter, gateway-httproute source, and TXT ownership.

evidence.summary: ExternalDNS IRSA and hosted-zone permissions are declared in Terraform.
Platform stack wires ExternalDNS to the public hosted zone and domain filter.

controls.title: ExternalDNS is scoped to the public hosted zone
controls.objective: Allow DNS automation only for the authorized domain and hosted zone with explicit ownership controls.

controls.rationale: Allow the storage controller to manage EBS volumes through a dedicated identity with complete trust restrictions.
controls.boundarySummary: Allow the named actors to perform the intended action only through the controlled workflow; deny bypasses, broad privileges, and unreviewed changes.
controls.implementedSummary: The EBS CSI add-on uses a dedicated IRSA role and AWS managed policy.
controls.gapSummary: The trust policy currently restricts sub but lacks aud=sts.amazonaws.com condition.

evidence.summary: EKS module configures the EBS CSI add-on and service account role.
PostgreSQL StatefulSet relies on Kubernetes storage provisioning.

controls.title: EBS CSI driver IRSA is missing aud condition
controls.objective: Allow the storage controller to manage EBS volumes through a dedicated identity with complete trust restrictions.

controls.rationale: Default application pods should not inherit AWS or Kubernetes API permissions unless a dedicated least-privilege identity is declared.
controls.boundarySummary: Allow the named actors to perform the intended action only through the controlled workflow; deny bypasses, broad privileges, and unreviewed changes.
controls.implementedSummary: Application manifests do not declare app IRSA, so workloads do not receive AWS API permissions by default. The app manifests also do not create Role, RoleBinding, or ClusterRoleBinding for app workloads.
controls.gapSummary: Workloads do not yet use dedicated service accounts and do not set automountServiceAccountToken: false to make the no-Kubernetes-API-token posture explicit.

evidence.summary: Application deployments are defined without IRSA annotations or dedicated service account controls.
Frontend deployment is part of the app workload set.
Kustomize deploys app manifests through GitOps.

controls.title: Application workloads have no AWS permissions but still need explicit service accounts
controls.objective: Default application pods should not inherit AWS or Kubernetes API permissions unless a dedicated least-privilege identity is declared.

controls.rationale: Prevent arbitrary namespaces from attaching public routes or mutating DNS records outside the authorized domain.
controls.boundarySummary: Allow the named actors to perform the intended action only through the controlled workflow; deny bypasses, broad privileges, and unreviewed changes.
controls.implementedSummary: The shared Gateway listener only accepts routes from namespaces labeled for public-gateway access; vintage, argocd, and monitoring namespaces are explicitly labeled. DNS mutation is limited to the configured public hosted zone.

evidence.summary: Gateway listener restricts route attachment with namespace selectors.
Application namespace declares the public gateway access label.
ExternalDNS is constrained to the configured hosted zone and domain.

controls.title: Public Gateway route attachment is namespace-scoped
controls.objective: Prevent arbitrary namespaces from attaching public routes or mutating DNS records outside the authorized domain.

controls.rationale: Limit GitOps reconciliation to authorized source paths, namespaces, and resource kinds.
controls.boundarySummary: Allow the named actors to perform the intended action only through the controlled workflow; deny bypasses, broad privileges, and unreviewed changes.
controls.implementedSummary: Cluster bootstrap creates separate hiraya-platform and hiraya-workloads AppProjects. The root app syncs gitops/clusters/dev/root from main with prune/selfHeal, and the vintage workload Application uses the hiraya-workloads project limited to configured workload namespaces.
controls.gapSummary: The platform AppProject remains intentionally broad, and the workload AppProject still allows all namespaced resource kinds; no resource-kind denylist or admission policy blocks privileged workload manifests.

evidence.summary: Bootstrap creates platform and workload AppProjects plus the root app-of-apps Application.
Vintage workload Application is assigned to the hiraya-workloads AppProject.
Platform Applications are assigned to the hiraya-platform AppProject.
GitOps tree defines the synced workload desired state.

controls.title: Argo CD GitOps boundary uses AppProjects but still allows broad resource kinds
controls.objective: Limit GitOps reconciliation to authorized source paths, namespaces, and resource kinds.

controls.rationale: Allow GitOps to create application resources while preventing privileged pods, high-privilege RBAC, or unauthorized cloud identities.
controls.boundarySummary: Allow the named actors to perform the intended action only through the controlled workflow; deny bypasses, broad privileges, and unreviewed changes.
controls.implementedSummary: GitOps creates Deployments, Services, StatefulSets, HTTPRoutes, ServiceMonitors, and other application resources.
controls.gapSummary: No policy/RBAC layer yet blocks high-privilege RBAC, privileged pods, or unauthorized service accounts.

evidence.summary: Kustomize includes application, database, route, dashboard, and secret resources.
Application resources include monitoring custom resources.
Existing validation policy pattern could be extended to app resource guardrails.

controls.title: Application resource creation lacks admission/RBAC policy guardrails
controls.objective: Allow GitOps to create application resources while preventing privileged pods, high-privilege RBAC, or unauthorized cloud identities.
